action.reasoning: Observation: The predict.py script is failing due to a model architecture mismatch. The error shows that the checkpoint file (final_model.pt) contains tensors with different shapes than what the current model expects. Specifically, the checkpoint has larger hidden dimensions (128, 64) while the current model expects smaller dimensions (64, 32). This happened because I modified the model architecture in the latest iteration but am still trying to load a checkpoint from the previous architecture. I need to either retrain the model with the current architecture or update the predict.py script to match the checkpoint's architecture. Since I have multiple checkpoints saved (including epoch-specific ones), I should identify which one matches the current model architecture and update predict.py accordingly. Looking at the workspace files, I have several checkpoints: final_model.pt, model_epoch_1.pt, etc. I need to determine which checkpoint corresponds to the current model architecture.
